business.headline: AI is helping you invest. But should you trust it?From picking stocks to timing trades, AI is becoming your new investment guide. The real question is, should you trust it with your money?

Mandatory but delayed: Why third-party motor insurance claims take years to settleHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the fourth story in our series Motor Mess, which looks at issues in motor insurance, we spoke to industry experts to understand why claims take years to settle.

Air India to cut international flights till July amid fuel surge, airspace curbsAir India will cut international flights from June to July as rising ATF prices, airspace restrictions, and longer routes make several operations unprofitable, CEO Campbell Wilson said.

How best to tackle oil shock? Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy respondsIn an interview with India Today, Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy, Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the global energy shock and its impact on the Indian economy following the recent hikes in commercial LPG and anticipated domestic fuel price increases.

Not complying with trade deal: Trump hikes tariffs on cars, trucks from EU to 25%The move comes months after Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to a new trade deal in July 2025, which set a 15 per cent tariff on most goods, including automobiles and auto parts.

Petrol, diesel prices may be hiked by Rs 4-5 per litre, LPG by Rs 40-50: SourcesPetrol and diesel prices may increase by Rs four to five per litre, while domestic LPG cylinder prices could rise by Rs 40-50 per cylinder, according to government sources.

Fuel, LPG prices may go up, decision likely within 5-7 days: Government sourcesPetrol, diesel prices may rise by Rs 4–5 per litre and LPG by Rs 40–50 per cylinder, following a sharp jump in global crude oil prices driven by West Asia war.

Will your minimum pension rise from Rs 1,000 to Rs 7,500? Find out who benefitsPensioners across the country may soon see a shift in their monthly income if the proposed changes go through. Here's what we know so far and who could benefit.

8th Pay Commission to visit Hyderabad, Srinagar, Ladakh: What does this mean?The 8th Pay Commission is taking its discussions beyond Delhi, with visits planned to Hyderabad, Srinagar and Ladakh. But what does this outreach mean for government employees and pensioners awaiting revisions?

Paytm sees strong user trust and high merchant stickiness post PPBL actionBrokerage firms tracking the company said the RBI action on PPBL has limited direct impact on Paytm's core business operations, given the structural separation between the listed entity and the payments bank.

Earning Rs 50,000 a month? The right way to spend, save and investManaging money at Rs 50,000 a month isn't just about covering expenses, it's about making choices that shape your financial future. But are you allocating it in the right way?

From petrol to food, why could everything get costlier togetherA mix of higher oil prices, a weaker rupee and monsoon risks is putting pressure on India's economy. The overlap could lift household costs, limit policy choices and raise stagflation concerns.

GST at record Rs 2.43 lakh crore in April, but domestic demand growth slowsA major part of April's growth came from import-related revenues. GST collected on imports rose sharply by 25.8% to Rs 57,580 crore.

Workplace injury claims jump 31%: Are businesses doing enough?On Labour Day, as conversations around worker safety and rights take centre stage, a pressing question emerges—are businesses truly doing enough to protect their workforce? A recent report suggests concerns are growing.

Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

Commercial LPG up by nearly Rs 1,000: Why have prices increased?Commercial LPG cylinder prices have risen again as global oil tensions keep costs elevated. The move shields households for now but raises pressure on restaurants, hotels and small businesses.

Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked by Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

entertainment.headline: Samay Raina, Sunil Pal turn tension into viral comedy moment on Kapil Sharma show A recent episode of The Great Indian Kapil Show turned an off-screen feud into on-screen comedy. Sunil Pal and Samay Raina came face to face, and the internet can't stop talking about it.

SRK helps pregnant Deepika climb stairs on King set, fans call him gem A behind-the-scenes clip from the King set shows Shah Rukh Khan offering Deepika Padukone a hand between takes in South Africa. The moment has gone viral soon after leaked images from the film's set drew attention online.

Shakira takes over Rio: 2 million fans, fireworks and one epic show Shakira opened her free Copacabana Beach concert in Rio on a grand note. The night stood out for the huge turnout and her first live performance of Choka Choka with Anitta.

Raja Shivaji box office Day 2: Riteish's film holds firm despite slight dip Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji continued its strong box office run on Day 2 despite a slight dip. The Marathi version remained the main contributor, while the Hindi release posed a steady start.

Diljit Dosanjh stops Canada show midway, reacts to pro-Khalistan protest Diljit Dosanjh paused his concert in Calgary, Canada, to address protesters questioning his appearance on Jimmy Fallon amid floods in Punjab. He said he used the US platform to speak about Punjab and noted his team had supported flood-affected regions.

M for Megastars: Why Mammootty and Mohanlal still define Malayalam cinema Forty-plus years in business, Mammootty, 74, and Mohanlal, 65, remain the first names of Malayalam cinema. They've reunited in Patriot after 18 years and the film has opened big. Understanding how the two veterans stay relevant and dominant.

Rajpal Yadav loves Michael Jackson biopic, there's a Charlie Chaplin twist to it Comedian Rajpal Yadav praised Michael, the Michael Jackson biopic, calling it a "commendable tribute". There's a Charlie Chaplin connection to why Yadav loves the MJ film. Read here.

Lyricist-writer Prasoon Joshi appointed Prasar Bharati Chairman Prasoon Joshi has been appointed Chairman of Prasar Bharati. The lyricist-writer brings experience from films, advertising and his role as CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) chief.

Rishab Shetty meets Amit Shah, praises his understanding of culture, cinema Rishab Shetty described his meeting with Amit Shah as memorable, highlighting their discussion on cinema, culture and heritage. The actor also shared that the Home Minister appreciated Kantara and spoke about his upcoming projects.

Is Tara Sutaria dating Aditya Roy Kapur after Veer Pahariya split rumours? Is Tara Sutaria dating Aditya Roy Kapur? A new report suggests so, even as speculation continues around her reported split with Veer Pahariya.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do trailer: Ayushmann Khurrana juggles between 3 relationships The trailer of Pati Patni Aur Woh Do shows Ayushmann Khurrana as a man caught between multiple relationships, leading to confusion and comic situations. Also starring Sara Ali Khan, Rakul Preet Singh and Wamiqa Gabbi, the film is set to release on May 15, 2026.

Factcheck: Tabu upset with Akshay, Priyadarshan for edited role in Bhooth Bangla? Bhooth Bangla director Priyadarshan has cleared the air over reports that Tabu was displeased with him and lead actor Akshay Kumar for alleged edits in her role. Read here what he said.

Kiara Advani shares sun-kissed Maldives moments with Sidharth, daughter Saraayah Kiara Advani's Maldives trip with Sidharth Malhotra is all about love, laughter and motherhood, as she shares family moments with daughter Saraayah. From beach time to reading sessions, the actor offers a glimpse into her life as a new mother.

Diljit Dosanjh says I don't fear death at Canada concert, video sparks concern A video from Diljit Dosanjh's Canada concert captured him speaking about death, detachment and Punjab. His remarks drew concern online, as fans discussed trolling, pressure, and mental health.

Oscars 2027 rule change allows multiple International Feature entries per country Oscars 2027 will see major rule changes as the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences allows multiple acting nominations in the same category and expands international film eligibility.

Swara Bhasker hits back at postpartum trolls: My body isn't your business Swara Bhasker has responded to trolling over her postpartum weight after sharing a throwback post. She said she chose not to "bounce back" and criticised the pressure on women to meet beauty standards after childbirth.

Usha Mangeshkar says she feels lonely at home after sisters Lata and Asha's death Usha Mangeshkar spoke about the shock of losing sisters Lata Mangeshkar and Asha Bhosle. She said the silence at home has deepened her grief and loneliness.

Patriot box office Day 1: Mohanlal-Mammootty film posts strong global debut Patriot opened strongly in theatres, earning Rs 29.37 crore worldwide on its first day. The Mohanlal and Mammootty reunion, backed by solid Kerala and overseas numbers, drove the opening.

Raja Shivaji box office Day 1: Riteish's film records strong Marathi opening Raja Shivaji opened strongly on Maharashtra Day, collecting around Rs 11.35 crore domestically. Led by its Marathi version, the film set a new opening benchmark and drew solid occupancy across the state.

general news.headline: Election of rejection: Bengal BJP chief targets TMC day ahead of resultsState BJP president Samik Bhattacharya, speaking to IndiaToday.in ahead of the Assembly results, called it an "election of rejection" of Mamata Banerjee. He projected 200-plus seats and framed the campaign around Bengali pride, identity, and a "Bengal first" sentiment.

1-year-old among 9 killed in Delhi residential building fire, AC blast suspectedThe bodies of the nine people killed in the blaze were recovered from the second floor of the building, while three others were found in the staircase. The body of one other person was recovered from the first floor.

Ball in US court, says Iran after Trump hints he may reject Tehran's new proposalIran has sent a new 14-point peace proposal to the United States via Pakistan. Trump said he would review it but suggested Tehran had not yet paid a big enough price.

Zero electricity bill, credit for selling surplus: Neighbour's envy driving solar in IndiaHouseholds across India are turning to rooftop solar through an unusual mix of triggers. From Varanasi to Greater Noida to a village in Maharashtra, neighbours are inspiring each other to go solar. Backed by government subsidies, growing awareness, and most importantly, lower electricity bills, solar is now powering more middle-class households in India than ever.

Only 3% in IITs, IIMs, NITs but they take 50% of India's higher education budgetHandcrafted ByOur Editors A tiny elite in IITs, IIMs and NITs commands nearly half of India's higher education budget. The remaining 96% of students are left to navigate a vastly underfunded system spread across hundreds of institutions. This isn't just unequal spending, it's a pipeline that decides whose futures get priority.

3-year-old boy raped, murdered by minor in Pune, body found stuffed in suitcaseThe juvenile was allegedly trying to flee Pune when he was caught by the police outside the city's railway station. He has confessed to the crimes, the police said. The minor had allegedly lured the toddler to his home with the promise of chocolates.

I was harassed by Brij Bhushan, I am one of the six victims: Vinesh PhogatOlympian Vinesh Phogat, a key face of the 2023 New Delhi protests, has identified herself as one of the six women harassed by Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, alleging upcoming trials for Asian Games 2026 are rigged to block her comeback.

Wheat grain cheaper now, but your atta, bread prices haven't dipped. Why?While wheat prices have gone down in mandis, there is no resultant relief in atta and bread prices for households. What's behind the mismatch between the rates at which farmers sell wheat and the retail prices of flour? Who is really benefiting?

Diljit Dosanjh stops Canada show midway, reacts to pro-Khalistan protestDiljit Dosanjh paused his concert in Calgary, Canada, to address protesters questioning his appearance on Jimmy Fallon amid floods in Punjab. He said he used the US platform to speak about Punjab and noted his team had supported flood-affected regions.

India's AC dilemma: How it cools the heat and heats the planetThere lies a painful paradox at the heart of India's cooling crisis. The very machines helping people survive extreme heat also contribute to the warming, making that heat worse.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Charred remains, blackened walls: Video reveals tragic aftermath of Delhi fireA video from inside the residential building shows the charred remains of the victims and the burnt interior of the structure. The bodies of five of the nine people killed in the blaze were recovered from the second floor of the building.
The Vibhuti Effect: Why do India's chess prodigies swear by the sacred ash?Why India's chess prodigies carry an ancient ritual into the world's most cerebral arena, and what it's doing to their game.

4-year-old Pune girl raped, murdered by elderly man, body dumped in cowshedPolice said a four-year-old girl was allegedly raped and murdered in Pune district, and a 65-year-old man with a criminal record has been arrested. Locals staged protests, demanding strict punishment for the accused.

Passenger opens emergency exit of moving flight at Chennai airport, arrestedAn Air Arabia passenger opened an emergency exit door while the aircraft was taxiing at Chennai International Airport. The pilot halted the flight, CISF personnel responded, and the passenger is being questioned.

Delhi judge found dead at home; family claims he fought with wife hours ago, criedA call to police, a locked bathroom, and a rushed hospital visit - what unfolded inside a Delhi home has left many questions unanswered as authorities begin probing the circumstances behind a judge's death.

Shakira takes over Rio: 2 million fans, fireworks and one epic showShakira opened her free Copacabana Beach concert in Rio on a grand note. The night stood out for the huge turnout and her first live performance of Choka Choka with Anitta.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Repolling in Bengal's Falta on May 21, EC flags subversion of democratic processIn an official statement, the Election Commission said that repolling will take place in all 285 booths across Falta Assembly Consitituency on May 24. The move came after the BJP alleged large-scale EVM tampering in the area.

Indian startup launches Mission Drishti aboard Elon Musk's Falcon-9GalaxEye Space launched Mission Drishti aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 on Sunday. The satellite brings OptoSAR imaging to India's private space push with all-weather, round-the-clock Earth observation.
Reddit or ChatGPT, who should you turn to for life advice?With Reddit and AI tools like ChatGPT becoming go-to sources for life advice, we explore which one people should turn to.
AI is helping you invest. But should you trust it?From picking stocks to timing trades, AI is becoming your new investment guide. The real question is, should you trust it with your money?

Isro in talks with Russia for semi-cryogenic engines for next-gen rocketIsro is advancing technical talks with Roscosmos to procure semi-cryogenic rocket engines. The proposed deal could support heavier launches and complement India's ongoing engine development.

Forget Rs 40 crore retirement fund, Indian students can't even save Rs 3 croreHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's Rs 40 crore retirement estimate by Sandeep Jethwani may be mathematically sound, but salary data tells a different story. With most graduates earning modest incomes and job stability still a concern, reports from PLFS, Unstop, and income surveys reveal a widening gap between what Indians earn and what they need to retire.

sports.headline: I was harassed by Brij Bhushan, I am one of the six victims: Vinesh Phogat Olympian Vinesh Phogat, a key face of the 2023 New Delhi protests, has identified herself as one of the six women harassed by Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, alleging upcoming trials for Asian Games 2026 are rigged to block her comeback.

Hardik is a plastic captain: Ex-selector slams MI skipper's intent in CSK loss Kris Srikkanth tore into Hardik Pandya after Mumbai Indians' outing against Chennai Super Kings at Chepauk. The criticism centred on Pandya's 18 off 23 balls and CSK's method of tying him down through the middle overs.

The Vibhuti Effect: Why do India's chess prodigies swear by the sacred ash? Why India's chess prodigies carry an ancient ritual into the world's most cerebral arena, and what it's doing to their game.

Has Hardik lost MI dressing room?EX-IND batter's big claim after CSK defeat Manoj Tiwary said Hardik Pandya appears to have lost the Mumbai Indians dressing room after the defeat to Chennai Super Kings. He said the side looked fundamentally unsettled, with limited backing for the captain and inconsistent performances damaging the campaign.

No longer CSK's Rs. 14.2 Crore gamble, Kartik Sharma spreads his wings in yellow Kartik Sharma finally proved that he wasn't just a big-money gamble for CSK, repaying the faith of the management by scoring a well-composed fifty to get his side the win in the El Clasico against Mumbai.

IPL 2026: Punjab's bowling in focus as Gujarat look to spice up playoffs race The IPL 2026 playoff race is heating up as Gujarat Titans look to keep their momentum going against the in-form Punjab Kings. Sunrisers Hyderabad will aim to edge closer to a spot in the playoffs as they come up against a resurgent KKR side.

From T20 World Cup heroes to IPL 2026 flops: Will MI rest their Indian core? Mahela Jayawardene has defended Mumbai Indians' Indian core after another defeat deepened their poor IPL 2026 run. With Bumrah, Hardik Pandya, Tilak Varma and Suryakumar Yadav struggling, MI are clinging to a mathematical chance of qualification.

IPL Play of the Day: Overton becomes death overs ace, leaves MI high and dry IPL 2026, CSK vs MI: From having very little experience in bowling at the death, Jamie Overton has evolved significantly, and that improvement was evident when he choked the Mumbai Indians batters in the El Clasico on Saturday at the MA Chidambaram Stadium.

Mumbai Indians can still qualify for the IPL 2026 playoffs. Here's how IPL 2026, CSK vs MI: Hardik Pandya's Mumbai Indians can still make their way through to the playoffs despite their heavy eight-wicket defeat to the Chennai Super Kings on Saturday at the MA Chidambaram Stadium.

No Lakshya. No party: India blown away by France 0-3 in semi-finals Thomas Cup: India were blown away by France in the semi-finals of Thomas Cup on Saturday, May 2. With Lakshya Sen forced to sit out of the tie, India lost 0-3 in straight games against the French.

Kartik Sharma recreates MS Dhoni's gun celebration after maiden IPL fifty vs MI Kartik Sharma's pre-planned gun celebration after his maiden IPL fifty against Mumbai Indians sent Chepauk into a Dhoni flashback on Saturday night.

Watch: Jamie Overton fiery pace breaks Tilak Varma's fitness band at ChepaukJamie Overton's searing pace had a painful consequence for Tilak Varma at Chepauk on Saturday, as a thunderous delivery snapped the Mumbai batter's fitness band clean off his wrist.

CSK complete league double over arch rivals MI with eight-wicket rout at ChepaukCSK vs MI, IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings outplayed Mumbai Indians at Chepauk to win by eight wickets and complete the league double. The result left Mumbai staring at a must-win finish with their playoff hopes hanging by a thread.

Marta Kostyuk caps off dream Madrid Open title with backflip celebration: WatchMarta Kostyuk clinched her maiden Madrid Open title by defeating Mirra Andreeva 6-3, 7-5, extending her unbeaten clay run to 12 matches. After the win, she celebrated with a stunning backflip, reflecting how much the title meant to her.

PBKS make heartwarming gesture for elderly fan struck by ball in MullanpurPunjab Kings invited fan Krishan Chand to their Dharamsala match after he was injured by a six in Mullanpur, with Priyansh Arya and Preity Zinta reaching out in a heartfelt gesture.

Babar Azam as captain again? Pakistan star opens up on fresh prospectsBabar Azam has kept the door open for a return as Pakistan captain, stating he will do what's best for the team. Under Babar, Peshawar Zalmi have advanced to the PSL final where they will face Marnus Labuschagne's Hyderabad Kingsmen.

BCCI set to review Impact Player rule after end of IPL 2026: SaikiaThe Indian Cricket Board will be reviewing the Impact Player rule in the Indian Premier League. The rule, which allows teams one substitution per match, has seen a lot of flak from players and experts.

Who is Raghu Sharma? Punjab leg-spinner makes IPL debut for MI in El ClasicoIPL 2026, CSK vs MI: Raghu Sharma made his IPL debut for Mumbai Indians against Chennai Super Kings at the MA Chidambaram Stadium, receiving his maiden cap from Hardik Pandya amid MI's struggling season.

Thrown off a train in January, Kuldeep Kumar breaks pole vault national recordKuldeep Kumar, 21-year-old from Madhya Pradesh cleared 5.41m in Bhubaneswar to break the national pole vault record, months after being stranded at Panvel station for carrying his poles.

Semi-final Highlights: India knocked out by France after 0-3 lossIndia vs France Highlights, Thomas Cup semi-final badminton: It was not HS Prannoy's night. It was not Ayush Shetty's or Kidambi Srikanth's night. India lose 0-3 to France and get knocked out of the Thomas Cup. France on the other hand become only the 2nd European nation after Denmark to reach the final of the tournament.

Ferguson sees no wrong in Jamieson's wicket celebration of Vaibhav SooryvanshiIPL 2026: Lockie Ferguson defended Kyle Jamieson after the IPL cautioned him for his send-off to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The caution fed a wider debate over on-field aggression, while Ferguson also addressed Punjab Kings' bowling inconsistencies.

Radha, Yastika return in India's strong 15-member squad for Women's T20 World CupYastika Bhatia and Radha Yadav have returned as India named a strong 15-member squad for the Women's T20 World Cup and the T20I series against England. Kashvee Gautam and Amanjot Kaur missed out due to injuries. Nandani Sharma earned her maiden India call-up after her impressive showing for DC in WPL 2026.

Will Pathirana play for KKR against SRH? Bowling coach Southee revealsKolkata Knight Riders are yet to decide whether Matheesha Pathirana will play against Sunrisers Hyderabad. Tim Southee said the call will depend on conditions, overseas balance and matchday selection.

No Lakshya Sen as India face tricky France in semi-finalsThomas Cup 2026: India's top-ranked badminton player Lakshya Sen is out of the semi-finals clash against France. This comes as a huge set-back for the Indian side who would have wanted Lakshya to play the first singles match against a tricky opponent.

Is MS Dhoni back for CSK? Ex-teammate Suresh Raina spills the beans ahead of MI clashIPL 2026: MS Dhoni could return for CSK against MI, with Suresh Raina hinting at a possible comeback after his injury layoff. Coach Michael Hussey confirmed Dhoni is fit to bat and keep, with only his running fitness delaying a return.

Went to India's dressing room: Pak captain Agha recalls Asia Cup handshake rowSalman Ali Agha revealed that the Pakistan team went to meet Indian players and shake hands with them but found the dressing room door shut. India and Pakistan have maintained the no-handshake policy since the 2025 Asia Cup.

Why did Kohli go unpunished? Fans back Jamieson over Sooryavanshi celebrationKyle Jamieson was warned and given a demerit point after his charged send-off to 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the IPL. The episode reopened questions over how the league polices aggression, celebration and consistency in its conduct rules.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi at 15 is the big prize and Jamieson's send-off proves itKyle Jamieson dismissed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi with a yorker and gave him a fiery send-off in Jaipur. Rajasthan Royals batting coach Vikram Rathour said the reaction showed how prized the 15-year-old's wicket has become in cricket.

Can India beat giant-slayers France? All you need to know about Thomas Cup semisThomas Cup 2026 semi-finals: India will have their work cut out on Saturday, May 2, as they take on giant-killers France in the semi-final of the Thomas Cup 2026. With a strong singles line-up, France will be up for the test but India will once again bank on Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty and Satwik-Chirag to step up and deliver the win.

PSG-Bayern, What a disaster game: Guardiola attends Stockport game, mocks UCL clashPep Guardiola joked about skipping PSG vs Bayern's 9-goal thriller, calling it a "disaster game" as he instead watched Stockport County in League One. He also brushed off fixture congestion talk as Manchester City chase Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
